SETTINGS$CONDENSER_MAX_SIZE_TOOLTIP: After this many events, the condenser will summarize history. Minimum 20.
ANALYTICS$DESCRIPTION: We use tools to understand how our application is used to improve your experience. You can enable or disable analytics. Your preferences will be stored and can be updated anytime.
SCHEMA$CONDENSER$MAX_SIZE$DESCRIPTION: Maximum number of events kept before the condenser runs.
SCHEMA$CONFIRMATION_MODE$DESCRIPTION: Require user confirmation before executing risky actions.
SCHEMA$LLM$API_KEY$DESCRIPTION: API key for authenticating with the LLM provider.
SCHEMA$LLM$AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID$DESCRIPTION: AWS access key ID for authentication.
SCHEMA$LLM$AWS_ROLE_NAME$DESCRIPTION: AWS role name for assuming roles.
SCHEMA$LLM$AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY$DESCRIP: AWS secret access key for authentication.
SCHEMA$LLM$AWS_SESSION_TOKEN$DESCRIPTION: AWS session token for temporary credentials.
SCHEMA$LLM$DISABLE_VISION$DESCRIPTION: Disable image processing for vision-capable models.
SCHEMA$LLM$ENABLE_ENCRYPTED_REASONING$DE: Request encrypted reasoning content in Responses API.
SCHEMA$LLM$EXTENDED_THINKING_BUDGET$DESC: Budget tokens for extended thinking (Anthropic models).
SCHEMA$LLM$EXTRA_HEADERS$DESCRIPTION: Optional HTTP headers to forward to LiteLLM requests.
SCHEMA$LLM$INPUT_COST_PER_TOKEN$DESCRIPT: Cost per input token for logging purposes.
SCHEMA$LLM$LITELLM_EXTRA_BODY$DESCRIPTIO: Additional parameters to pass to LiteLLM requests.
SCHEMA$LLM$LOG_COMPLETIONS_FOLDER$DESCRI: Folder to log LLM completions to.
SCHEMA$LLM$MAX_MESSAGE_CHARS$DESCRIPTION: Approximate maximum characters in each event/content sent to the LLM.
SCHEMA$LLM$MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS$DESCRIPTION: Maximum number of output tokens the model may generate.
SCHEMA$LLM$MODEL_CANONICAL_NAME$DESCRIPT: Canonical model name for feature registry lookups.
SCHEMA$LLM$NUM_RETRIES$DESCRIPTION: Number of retry attempts for failed requests.
SCHEMA$LLM$OUTPUT_COST_PER_TOKEN$DESCRIP: Cost per output token for logging purposes.
SCHEMA$LLM$REASONING_EFFORT$DESCRIPTION: The effort to put into reasoning (low, medium, high, xhigh, or none).
SCHEMA$LLM$REASONING_SUMMARY$DESCRIPTION: Level of detail for reasoning summaries (auto, concise, or detailed).
SCHEMA$LLM$RETRY_MAX_WAIT$DESCRIPTION: Maximum wait time between retries in seconds.
SCHEMA$LLM$RETRY_MIN_WAIT$DESCRIPTION: Minimum wait time between retries in seconds.
SCHEMA$LLM$RETRY_MULTIPLIER$DESCRIPTION: Multiplier for exponential backoff between retries.
SCHEMA$LLM$STREAM$DESCRIPTION: Enable streaming responses from the LLM.
SCHEMA$LLM$TEMPERATURE$DESCRIPTION: Controls randomness in model responses. Lower values are more deterministic.
SCHEMA$LLM$TOP_K$DESCRIPTION: Limits token selection to the K most likely tokens.
SCHEMA$LLM$TOP_P$DESCRIPTION: Controls nucleus sampling by limiting token selection to the smallest set whose cumulative probability exceeds this value.
SCHEMA$LLM$USAGE_ID$DESCRIPTION: Unique usage identifier for the LLM.
SCHEMA$MAX_ITERATIONS$DESCRIPTION: Maximum number of iterations the conversation will run before stopping.
SCHEMA$MCP_CONFIG$DESCRIPTION: MCP server configuration for the agent.
SCHEMA$SECURITY_ANALYZER$DESCRIPTION: Security analyzer that evaluates actions before execution.
SCHEMA$VERIFICATION$CONFIRMATION_MODE$DE: Require user confirmation before executing risky actions.
SCHEMA$VERIFICATION$CRITIC_ENABLED$DESCR: Enable critic evaluation for the agent.
SCHEMA$VERIFICATION$CRITIC_THRESHOLD$DES: Critic success threshold used for iterative refinement.
SCHEMA$VERIFICATION$ENABLE_ITERATIVE_REF: Automatically retry tasks when critic scores fall below the threshold.
SCHEMA$VERIFICATION$MAX_REFINEMENT_ITERA: Maximum number of refinement attempts after critic feedback.
SCHEMA$VERIFICATION$SECURITY_ANALYZER$DE: Security analyzer that evaluates actions before execution.

BITBUCKET_DATA_CENTER$TOKEN_HELP_TEXT: Create an <0>HTTP access token</0> in your Bitbucket Data Center instance with repository read/write and pull request read/write permissions. For personal access tokens, use the format 'username:token'. For project tokens, use the format 'x-token-auth:your-token'.
AGENT_ERROR$ERROR_ACTION_NOT_EXECUTED_ST: Pause button pressed. Agent is stopped. The action has not been executed.
AGENT_ERROR$ERROR_ACTION_NOT_EXECUTED_ER: The action has not been executed due to a runtime error. The runtime system may have crashed and restarted due to resource constraints. Any previously established system state, dependencies, or environment variables may have been lost.

TIPS$CUSTOMIZE_MICROAGENT: You can customize OpenHands for your repo using an available microagent. Ask OpenHands to put a description of the repo, including how to run the code, into .openhands/microagents/repo.md.

CONVERSATION$NO_HOOKS: No hooks configured for this conversation.
SKILLS_MODAL$WARNING: If you update the skills, you will need to stop the conversation and then click on the refresh button to see the changes.
COMMON$FETCH_ERROR: Failed to fetch skills. Please try again later.
TIPS$SETUP_SCRIPT: You can add .openhands/setup.sh to your repository to automatically run a setup script every time you start an OpenHands conversation.
TIPS$VSCODE_INSTANCE: Every OpenHands conversation comes with a VS Code instance, where you can interact with the development environment.
TIPS$SAVE_WORK: Be sure to regularly save your work, either by pushing to GitHub or by downloading your files via VS Code.
TIPS$SPECIFY_FILES: When possible, include the names of files or functions OpenHands should focus on. This can help OpenHands work faster, save money, and improve accuracy.
TIPS$HEADLESS_MODE: You can run OpenHands in headless mode to create automations, like responding to 500 errors by automatically creating a fix.
TIPS$CLI_MODE: You can run OpenHands as a CLI, similar to Claude Code.
TIPS$GITHUB_HOOK: OpenHands Cloud offers a GitHub hook, so you can say "@openhands fix the merge conflicts" or "@openhands fix the feedback on this PR" right inside the GitHub UI.
TIPS$BLOG_SIGNUP: Sign up for the OpenHands Blog to hear about new features and the latest releases.
TIPS$API_USAGE: OpenHands has an API! Create OpenHands conversations with simple cURL command.

PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$CONFIGURE_MODAL_DESCR: Important: Make sure the workspace integration for your target workspace is already configured. Check the documentation for more information.
Important: Check the documentation for more information about configuring the workspace integration or updating an existing integration.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$WORKSPACE_NAME_HINT: Workspace name can be found in the browser URL when you're accessing a resource (eg: issue) in {{platform}}.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_ADMIN_TOKEN_P: Paste a short-lived Jira admin PAT
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_ADMIN_TOKEN_H: Used once to call Jira's webhook API, then discarded. This does not link or verify the Jira admin user in OpenHands. Use manual setup if you cannot share an admin PAT.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_EXISTING_ADMI: Enter a Jira admin PAT to reinstall or refresh the webhook. It is used once for Jira's webhook API and never stored.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_MANUAL_PREPAR: Generate webhook details to save this configuration and create a connection-specific webhook URL and signing secret.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_MANUAL_INSTRU: Create a global webhook in Jira using the URL and secret below. Subscribe to comment_created and jira:issue_updated. After you save it in Jira, click I created the webhook so OpenHands starts listening.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_MANUAL_UPDATE: Update the global Jira webhook with these values, then click Update. This rotates the signing secret stored in OpenHands.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_REMOVE_HELP: To also delete the Jira webhook, enter a Jira admin PAT when you confirm below. Without it, only OpenHands is disabled.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_REMOVE_HELP_M: To also delete the Jira webhook, switch back to admin PAT setup and enter a Jira admin PAT before removing. Otherwise only OpenHands is disabled.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_REMOVE_WITH_R: Remove this Jira Data Center integration and delete the Jira webhook? The admin PAT will be used once and will not be stored.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_REMOVE_WITHOU: Remove this Jira Data Center integration without deleting the Jira webhook? Jira may keep sending webhook deliveries until you delete it manually.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$WORKSPACE_NAME_VALIDA: Workspace name can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores

COMMON$JUPYTER_EMPTY_MESSAGE: Your Jupyter notebook is empty. No cells to display.
HOME$NO_REPOSITORY_FOUND: No repository found to launch conversation
CONVERSATION$FAILED_TO_START_FROM_TASK: Failed to start the conversation from task.
CONVERSATION$NOT_EXIST_OR_NO_PERMISSION: This conversation does not exist, or you do not have permission to access it. If this is your conversation, try switching to the workspace where it was created.
COMMON$CODE_AGENT_DESCRIPTION: Write, edit, and debug with AI assistance in real time.
COMMON$PLAN_AGENT_DESCRIPTION: Outline goals, structure tasks, and map your next steps.

ORG$INVITE_MEMBERS_ERROR: Failed to send invitations. Please try again.

HOOKS_MODAL$WARNING: Hooks are loaded from your workspace. This view refreshes on demand and may differ from the hooks that were active when the conversation started. Stop and restart the conversation to apply changes.
ORG$INVITATION_ACCEPT_DESCRIPTION: You have been invited to join an organization. Would you like to accept this invitation?

CTA$ENTERPRISE_DESCRIPTION: Cloud allows you to access OpenHands anywhere and coordinate with your team like never before.
ENTERPRISE$GET_OPENHANDS_SUBTITLE: Cloud allows you to access OpenHands anywhere and coordinate with your team like never before.
ENTERPRISE$SAAS_DESCRIPTION: Access OpenHands in the cloud. No setup required—start coding with your team from anywhere.
ENTERPRISE$SELF_HOSTED_DESCRIPTION: Deploy OpenHands on your own infrastructure. Full control over your data, compliance, and security. Ideal for enterprises that require on-premises or private cloud deployment.
ENTERPRISE$SELF_HOSTED_CARD_DESCRIPTION: Deploy OpenHands on your own infrastructure. Full control over data, compliance, and security.
ENTERPRISE$FORM_SAAS_SUBTITLE: Tell us about your team and we'll help you get started.
ENTERPRISE$FORM_SELF_HOSTED_SUBTITLE: Tell us about your needs and we'll be in touch.

ENTERPRISE$FORM_MESSAGE_SELF_HOSTED_PLAC: Tell us about your deployment needs...
ENTERPRISE$REQUEST_SUBMITTED_DESCRIPTION: Your request has been submitted. We will follow up with next steps shortly.
ORG$GIT_CONVERSATION_ROUTING_DESCRIPTION: Claim organizations so resolver requests route to the appropriate OpenHands org for shared visibility, auditing, and ownership. If a requester is not a member of the claiming org, the conversation falls back to their Personal Workspace. Available organizations are derived from your connected GitHub/GitLab identity. Only organization admins and owners can manage organization claims.
ORG$CLAIM_ERROR: Connection failed. Org is already claimed.
SETTINGS$AGENT_PAGE_DESCRIPTION: Choose which agent handles your conversations. ACP runs an external agent subprocess that you configure here.
SETTINGS$AGENT_COMMAND_HINT: The full subprocess command to start the ACP agent. Whitespace-separated. Add API keys and credentials via the Secrets panel.
SETTINGS$AGENT_MODEL_HINT: Optional. Passed through to the ACP agent as its model override.
SETTINGS$AGENT_DISABLED_TOOLTIP: This setting is disabled when using {{agentName}}
SETTINGS$PROFILES_DESCRIPTION: Profiles are created automatically when you save a configuration on the LLM page. The newest one becomes active; switch, rename, or delete saved profiles from this list.
SETTINGS$PROFILES_EMPTY: No profiles saved yet. Configure a model on the LLM page — it will be saved here automatically.
SETTINGS$PROFILES_LOAD_ERROR: Failed to load profiles. Please try again.
SETTINGS$PROFILE_DELETE_CONFIRMATION: Delete profile "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
SETTINGS$PROFILE_NAME_RULE: Name must be 1–64 characters: letters, digits, dot, dash, or underscore.
CRITIC$ITERATIVE_REFINEMENT_HINT: Want the agent to automatically fix potential issues? Enable iterative refinement in settings.
BITBUCKET_DATA_CENTER$WEBHOOK_INSTALL_FA: Failed to install Bitbucket Data Center webhook
BITBUCKET_DATA_CENTER$WEBHOOK_UNINSTALL_: Failed to uninstall Bitbucket Data Center webhook
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_HOST_HELP: Hostname only, e.g. jira.yourcompany.com — don't include https://.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_WEBHOOK_MODE_: Set it up in Jira myself
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_SERVICE_ACCOU: OpenHands comments and reacts as this account.
Configured from the OpenHands Enterprise Admin Console.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_SVC_ACC_API_P: Paste the service account's Jira PAT
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_SVC_ACC_API_S: Saved — leave blank to keep the current token
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_REMOVE_ADMIN_: Enter an admin PAT to delete the Jira webhook from Jira. Leave blank to remove only the OpenHands integration.
Required to remove the integration and delete the Jira webhook. It is used once for Jira's webhook API and never stored.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_INTEGRATION_O: Webhook setup is managed by the OpenHands user who created this integration.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_WEBHOOK_INSTA: OpenHands saved the Jira Data Center integration, but could not install the Jira webhook. Check the Jira admin PAT or set up the webhook manually.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_WEBHOOK_REMOV: OpenHands removed the Jira Data Center integration, but could not delete the Jira webhook in Jira. Check the Jira admin PAT or remove it manually.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_PAUSE_HELP: On means OpenHands responds to Jira events. Off pauses responses but keeps the Jira webhook installed.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$ACTIVE_TOGGLE_HELP: Turn off to keep the configuration but stop OpenHands from responding.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_PANEL_SUBTITL: Let OpenHands respond to @openhands mentions on your Jira Data Center issues.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_ADMIN_SUBTITL: Let OpenHands respond to @openhands mentions on your Jira Data Center issues. NOTE: To let agents read and query Jira mid-conversation, enable OAuth mode in the OHE Admin Config and redeploy or have users add a Jira token as a Secret.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_MEMBER_LINK_P: To connect, mention @openhands on any Jira Data Center issue, or link your account now.
PROJECT_MANAGEMENT$JIRA_DC_ASK_ADMIN: Ask an admin to configure Jira Data Center.
SETTINGS$ACP_CREDENTIALS_DESCRIPTION: API key and optional base URL for this provider. Saved as global secrets and injected into the agent subprocess at runtime.
SETTINGS$ACP_SECRET_API_KEY_HINT: API key for this provider. Leave blank to use a subscription login or an env var already set on the backend.
SETTINGS$ACP_SECRET_BASE_URL_HINT: Optional base URL override for proxies or alternative API gateways.
SETTINGS$ACP_SECRET_OAUTH_TOKEN_HINT: Subscription OAuth token for Claude Pro/Max. Run `claude setup-token` in your terminal to get it.
SETTINGS$ACP_SECRET_FILE_BLOB_HINT: Paste the full contents of {{file}}. Run `cat {{file}}` to get it. Saved as a secret and written to disk for the agent at runtime.
SETTINGS$ACP_SECRET_GCP_PROJECT_HINT: Google Cloud project ID for Vertex AI (e.g. the output of `gcloud config get-value project`).
SETTINGS$ACP_SECRET_GCP_LOCATION_HINT: Vertex AI region, for example us-central1.
SETTINGS$ACP_SECRET_VERTEXAI_FLAG_HINT: Set to true to route Gemini through Vertex AI using the credentials above.
ORG$EMAIL_DELIVERY_NOT_CONFIGURED: Email delivery is not configured on this instance. Share invite links with invitees directly.
ORG$INVITATIONS_CREATED_SHARE_LINKS: Invitations created. Share these links with the invitees:
ORG$AUTO_ADD_ENABLED_HINT: New users join this organization automatically when they sign in. Invitations are only needed to pre-assign a role.
SETTINGS$ENABLED_TOOLTIP: When on, this skill is available to the agent during conversations. When off, it is excluded.
SETTINGS$AUTO_LOAD_TOOLTIP: When on, this marketplace's plugins are loaded automatically at the start of every conversation. When off, the marketplace stays registered and its plugins can be loaded on demand.
SETTINGS$SKILLS_EMPTY_HINT: Skills will appear here once configured
SETTINGS$SKILLS_PERMISSIONS_DESCRIPTION: Configure which skills are enabled and auto-loaded
SETTINGS$CONNECT_REPOSITORIES_DESCRIPTIO: Add repository URLs to load skills from external sources
SETTINGS$MARKETPLACES_DESCRIPTION: Connect skill repositories from Git URLs, manage org-wide skill visibility, and set which skills are enabled by default in conversations
SETTINGS$CONNECT_MARKETPLACES_DESCRIPTIO: Register marketplaces — git repositories that provide plugins and skills. Turn on Auto-Load to load a marketplace's plugins automatically at the start of every conversation.
SETTINGS$ORG_SKILLS_DESCRIPTION: Manage skills available to your organization
SETTINGS$PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD_READONLY: A plugin's availability is controlled by its marketplace's Auto-Load setting.
SETTINGS$MARKETPLACE_REPO_PATH_INVALID: Repository Path must be a folder inside the repository (e.g., marketplaces/internal), not a URL or absolute path
SETTINGS$MARKETPLACE_SOURCE_READONLY: Source cannot be changed after creation
SETTINGS$MARKETPLACE_INSTANCE_READONLY: Instance marketplaces are configured by environment variables
SETTINGS$MARKETPLACE_ORG_REQUIRES_ADMIN: Only organization admins can modify this
SETTINGS$MARKETPLACE_ORG_REQUIRE_ADMIN: Organization admin or owner required to add org-level marketplace
SETTINGS$MARKETPLACE_NAME_INVALID: Name must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores
SETTINGS$DELETE_CONFIRMATION_MESSAGE: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
